title: Flag of Slovenia

infobox.value: [National flag](/wiki/National_flag "National flag") and [naval ensign](/wiki/Naval_ensign "Naval ensign") [Small vexillological symbol or pictogram in black and white showing the different uses of the flag](/wiki/File:FIAV_normal.svg "Vexillological description") [Small vexillological symbol or pictogram in black and white showing the different uses of the flag](/wiki/File:FIAV_111001.svg "National flag, war ensign")

A horizontal [tricolor](/wiki/Tricolour_(flag) "Tricolour (flag)") of [white](/wiki/White "White"), [blue](/wiki/Blue "Blue"), and [red](/wiki/Red "Red"); charged with the [coat of arms](/wiki/Coat_of_Arms_of_Slovenia "Coat of Arms of Slovenia") at the [hoist side](/wiki/Hoist_(flag) "Hoist (flag)")

([Slovene](/wiki/Slovene_language "Slovene language"): *zastava Slovenije*) features three equal horizontal bands of white (top), blue, and red, with the [coat of arms of Slovenia](/wiki/Coat_of_arms_of_Slovenia "Coat of arms of Slovenia") located in the upper hoist side of the flag centred in the white and blue bands. The coat of arms is a shield with the image of Mount [Triglav](/wiki/Triglav "Triglav"), [Slovenia](/wiki/Slovenia "Slovenia")'s highest peak, in white against a blue background at the centre; beneath it are two wavy blue lines representing the [Adriatic Sea](/wiki/Adriatic_Sea "Adriatic Sea") and local rivers, and above it are three six-pointed golden stars arranged in an inverted triangle which are taken from the coat of arms of the [Counts of Celje](/wiki/Counts_of_Celje "Counts of Celje"), the great Slovene dynastic house of the late 14th and early 15th centuries.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

| Design | A horizontal [tricolor](/wiki/Tricolour_(flag) "Tricolour (flag)") of [white](/wiki/White "White"), [blue](/wiki/Blue "Blue"), and [red](/wiki/Red "Red"); charged with the [coat of arms](/wiki/Coat_of_Arms_of_Slovenia "Coat of Arms of Slovenia") at the [hoist side](/wiki/Hoist_(flag) "Hoist (flag)") |

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f8/Slowenien-daenemark-4039-1439.jpg/250px-Slowenien-daenemark-4039-1439.jpg)](/wiki/File:Slowenien-daenemark-4039-1439.jpg)

Tram in [Vienna](/wiki/Vienna "Vienna") decorated with Slovene flag

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/0d/Defilada_Niepodleg%C5%82o%C5%9Bci_z_okazji_%C5%9Awi%C4%99ta_Wojska_Polskiego_15_sierpnia_2018_%286%29.jpg/250px-Defilada_Niepodleg%C5%82o%C5%9Bci_z_okazji_%C5%9Awi%C4%99ta_Wojska_Polskiego_15_sierpnia_2018_%286%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Defilada_Niepodleg%C5%82o%C5%9Bci_z_okazji_%C5%9Awi%C4%99ta_Wojska_Polskiego_15_sierpnia_2018_(6).jpg)

Slovenian soldiers parading with flag in [Warsaw](/wiki/Warsaw "Warsaw")

The national **flag of [Slovenia](/wiki/Slovenia "Slovenia")** ([Slovene](/wiki/Slovene_language "Slovene language"): *zastava Slovenije*) features three equal horizontal bands of white (top), blue, and red, with the [coat of arms of Slovenia](/wiki/Coat_of_arms_of_Slovenia "Coat of arms of Slovenia") located in the upper hoist side of the flag centred in the white and blue bands. The coat of arms is a shield with the image of Mount [Triglav](/wiki/Triglav "Triglav"), [Slovenia](/wiki/Slovenia "Slovenia")'s highest peak, in white against a blue background at the centre; beneath it are two wavy blue lines representing the [Adriatic Sea](/wiki/Adriatic_Sea "Adriatic Sea") and local rivers, and above it are three six-pointed golden stars arranged in an inverted triangle which are taken from the coat of arms of the [Counts of Celje](/wiki/Counts_of_Celje "Counts of Celje"), the great Slovene dynastic house of the late 14th and early 15th centuries.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

The Slovenian flag's colours are considered to be [pan-Slavic](/wiki/Pan-Slavism "Pan-Slavism"), but they actually come from the [medieval](/wiki/Middle_Ages "Middle Ages") coat of arms of the [Holy Roman Empire](/wiki/Holy_Roman_Empire "Holy Roman Empire")'s [duchy of Carniola](/wiki/Duchy_of_Carniola "Duchy of Carniola"), consisting of 3 stars, a mountain, and three colours (red, blue, yellow), [crescent](/wiki/Crescent "Crescent").[[3]](#cite_note-3) The existing [Slovene tricolour](/wiki/Slovene_tricolor "Slovene tricolor") was raised for the first time in history during the [Revolution of 1848](/wiki/Revolution_of_1848 "Revolution of 1848") by the Slovene [Romantic nationalist](/wiki/Romantic_nationalist "Romantic nationalist") activist and poet [Lovro Toman](/wiki/Lovro_Toman "Lovro Toman") on 7 April 1848, in [Ljubljana](/wiki/Ljubljana "Ljubljana"), in response to a German flag which was raised on top of [Ljubljana Castle](/wiki/Ljubljana_Castle "Ljubljana Castle").[[4]](#cite_note-Slovene_tricolor_RTVSLO-4) Similar colors and designs make up other [Slavic flags](/wiki/Slavic_flag "Slavic flag"), particularly the [flag of Slovakia](/wiki/Flag_of_Slovakia "Flag of Slovakia").

The [civil and state ensign](/wiki/Civil_ensign "Civil ensign") for ships has the same design as the national flag, but a different shape (2:3 instead of 1:2). Boats up to 24 metres (79 ft) use the national flag as an ensign.[[5]](#cite_note-5) The [naval jack](/wiki/Naval_jack "Naval jack") uses colours of the coat of arms, a white, blue, and yellow horizontal tricolour.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## Historical development

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Flag_of_Slovenia&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Historical development")]

### Origins

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Flag_of_Slovenia&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Origins")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/0a/Flag_of_the_Slovene_Nation.svg/250px-Flag_of_the_Slovene_Nation.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Flag_of_the_Slovene_Nation.svg)

1:2 [![Small vexillological symbol or pictogram in black and white showing the different uses of the flag](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/4b/FIAV_historical.svg/40px-FIAV_historical.svg.png)](/wiki/File:FIAV_historical.svg "Design used in the past, but now abandoned") Flag of the Slovene nation, first flown during the [spring of nations](/wiki/Spring_of_nations "Spring of nations") of 1848.

The white-blue-red Slovene flag was first raised on 7 April 1848, on a building between [Congress Square](/wiki/Congress_Square "Congress Square") and [Prešeren Square](/wiki/Pre%C5%A1eren_Square "Prešeren Square") in Ljubljana, by a group of nationally minded students led by the prominent [liberal nationalist](/wiki/Liberal_nationalist "Liberal nationalist") activist and poet [Lovro Toman](/wiki/Lovro_Toman "Lovro Toman"). Despite opposition from the local ethnic [Germans](/wiki/Germans "Germans") it was subsequently recognized by the [Austrian](/wiki/Austrian_Empire "Austrian Empire") Government as the official flag of Carniola. This formal recognition, albeit on a regional level, was an exception to the policy of the Austrian Government which tended to persecute [national symbols](/wiki/National_symbols "National symbols") of the non-German nationalities in the [Empire](/wiki/Austrian_Empire "Austrian Empire").

In addition, Austrian authorities saw all tricolors as basically [nationalist](/wiki/Nationalist "Nationalist") and potentially [revolutionary](/wiki/Revolutionary "Revolutionary") symbols, so Austrian provinces (as the Empire itself) were only allowed to use [bicolors](/w/index.php?title=Gallery_of_bicolor_flags&action=edit&redlink=1 "Gallery of bicolor flags (page does not exist)") (the only exception being the flag of the [Kingdom of Croatia and Slavonia](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Croatia_and_Slavonia "Kingdom of Croatia and Slavonia"), since it was interpreted to be a combination of the [Croatian](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Croatia_(Habsburg) "Kingdom of Croatia (Habsburg)") and [Slavonian](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Slavonia "Kingdom of Slavonia") bicolors). So the official recognition of the Carniolan white-blue-red tricolor instead of the traditional white-blue bicolor was seen as a major achievement by the [Slovenes](/wiki/Slovenes "Slovenes") and it quickly became the symbol representing the idea of [United Slovenia](/wiki/United_Slovenia "United Slovenia"). In the second half of the 19th century, the Slovene national tricolor became the only truly all-Slovene symbol, representing all Slovenes, regardless of the historical region in which they lived.

The tricolor flag continued to be associated with Slovenia during the country's incorporation into [Yugoslavia](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Yugoslavia "Kingdom of Yugoslavia"), although officially the whole kingdom including Slovenia had the same flag, in this case, the blue-white-red. In the [interwar](/wiki/Interwar_period "Interwar period") period, it was also used by the Slovenes of the [Julian March](/wiki/Julian_March "Julian March") that were annexed to [Italy](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Italy "Kingdom of Italy"), where it was prohibited and persecuted by the [fascist regime](/wiki/Italian_fascism "Italian fascism").

### Slovene flags during and after WWII

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Flag_of_Slovenia&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Slovene flags during and after WWII")]

During [World War II](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II") The Slovene [national colors](/wiki/National_colors "National colors") were used both by the [Partisan Resistance Movement](/wiki/Liberation_Front_of_the_Slovene_Nation "Liberation Front of the Slovene Nation") (usually with a red star in the middle) and by the [Slovene Home Guard](/wiki/Slovene_Home_Guard "Slovene Home Guard"), the voluntary [anti-Communist](/wiki/Anti-Communist "Anti-Communist") [militia](/wiki/Militia "Militia") sponsored and supported by the [Nazi German](/wiki/Nazi_German "Nazi German") occupation forces.

In 1945 a red star was officially placed on the flag of the [Socialist Republic of Slovenia](/wiki/Socialist_Republic_of_Slovenia "Socialist Republic of Slovenia"), a constituent of the [Socialist Yugoslavia](/wiki/Socialist_Yugoslavia "Socialist Yugoslavia").

* [![1:2 Flag used by Slovene Home Guard 1941–1945.](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/74/Flag_of_the_Slovene_Home_Guard.svg/250px-Flag_of_the_Slovene_Home_Guard.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Flag_of_the_Slovene_Home_Guard.svg "1:2 Flag used by Slovene Home Guard 1941–1945.")

  1:2 [![Small vexillological symbol or pictogram in black and white showing the different uses of the flag](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/4b/FIAV_historical.svg/40px-FIAV_historical.svg.png)](/wiki/File:FIAV_historical.svg "Design used in the past, but now abandoned") Flag used by [Slovene Home Guard](/wiki/Slovene_Home_Guard "Slovene Home Guard") 1941–1945.
* [![1:2 Flag used by Slovene Partisans that was adopted on 26 September 1941 at the Stolice meeting of the Yugoslav Partisans.](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/4f/Flag_of_the_Slovene_Partisans.svg/250px-Flag_of_the_Slovene_Partisans.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Flag_of_the_Slovene_Partisans.svg "1:2 Flag used by Slovene Partisans that was adopted on 26 September 1941 at the Stolice meeting of the Yugoslav Partisans.")

  1:2 [![Small vexillological symbol or pictogram in black and white showing the different uses of the flag](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/4b/FIAV_historical.svg/40px-FIAV_historical.svg.png)](/wiki/File:FIAV_historical.svg "Design used in the past, but now abandoned") Flag used by [Slovene Partisans](/wiki/Slovene_Partisans "Slovene Partisans") that was adopted on 26 September 1941 at the Stolice meeting of the Yugoslav Partisans.
* [![1:2 Flag of SR Slovenia, 1945–1991.](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/4a/Flag_of_Slovenia_%281945%E2%80%931991%29.svg/250px-Flag_of_Slovenia_%281945%E2%80%931991%29.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Flag_of_Slovenia_(1945%E2%80%931991).svg "1:2 Flag of SR Slovenia, 1945–1991.")

  1:2 [![Small vexillological symbol or pictogram in black and white showing the different uses of the flag](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/4b/FIAV_historical.svg/40px-FIAV_historical.svg.png)](/wiki/File:FIAV_historical.svg "Design used in the past, but now abandoned") Flag of SR Slovenia, 1945–1991.

### Flag of independent Slovenia

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Flag_of_Slovenia&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Flag of independent Slovenia")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d1/Civil_Ensign_of_Slovenia.svg/250px-Civil_Ensign_of_Slovenia.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Civil_Ensign_of_Slovenia.svg)

2:3 Civil ensign.

Following Slovene independence from [Yugoslavia](/wiki/Socialist_Federal_Republic_of_Yugoslavia "Socialist Federal Republic of Yugoslavia"), the red star was removed and the new coat of arms by [Marko Pogačnik](/wiki/Marko_Poga%C4%8Dnik "Marko Pogačnik") was added. The flag was officially adopted on 27 June 1991, following a long and controversial dispute about the coat of arms of the new Republic.

## Government (maritime) flags

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Flag_of_Slovenia&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Government (maritime) flags")]

These flags are used on naval vessels only.

| Flag | Use | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | [President of Slovenia](/wiki/President_of_Slovenia "President of Slovenia") | A white field with a tricolor border in white, blue and red. The [national coat of arms](/wiki/Coat_of_arms_of_Slovenia "Coat of arms of Slovenia") is at the center. |
|  | President of the [National Assembly of Slovenia](/wiki/National_Assembly_(Slovenia) "National Assembly (Slovenia)") | A red field with a blue border, with the national coat of arms at the center. |
|  | [Prime Minister of Slovenia](/wiki/Prime_Minister_of_Slovenia "Prime Minister of Slovenia") | A blue field with a red border, with the national coat of arms at the center. |
|  | [Minister of Defence of Slovenia](/wiki/Ministry_of_Defence_(Slovenia) "Ministry of Defence (Slovenia)") | A crimson field with an azure border, in the center is the [Slovenian Army emblem](/wiki/Slovenian_Armed_Forces "Slovenian Armed Forces"). |
|  | [Chief of the General Staff of Slovenia](/wiki/Chief_of_the_General_Staff_(Slovenia) "Chief of the General Staff (Slovenia)") | An azure field with a crimson border, the center contains the Slovenian Army emblem. |
